basic_details.job_title: Central Bank of India AGM Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 15 Assistant General Manager Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Central Bank of India (Human Capital Management - Recruitment & Promotion, Central Office)
basic_details.short_information: The latest Central Bank of India (CBI) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Assistant General Manager (AGM) - Specialist Category posts! Published on 30 April 2026 at www.centralbank.bank.in, this new recruitment drive offers 15 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 33 to 45 years age group and possessing qualifications such as CA/CFA/CMA, MBA/PGDM, B.E./B.Tech, PG degree/diploma, or relevant specialist certification with required experience can apply online from 30 April 2026 to 25 May 2026. Find all details about the CBI AGM Specialist new vacancy 2026, including eligibi

application_fee.details: Application fees / intimation charges are payable online from 30-04-2026 to 25-05-2026. The fee once paid is non-refundable. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

age_limit.details: Age limit for all posts is Minimum 33 years and Maximum 45 years as on 31-03-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines as follows:

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 31-03-2026. All educational qualifications must be from a university recognized by Govt. of India / approved by Govt. Regulatory Bodies. Any experience of less than 6 months in an organization shall not be considered for determining eligibility, except when such experience is part of current employment. The post-wise educational qualification and experience requirements are as follows: | Post-wise Educational Qualification, Experience and Age Limit

selection_process: Sr. No. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
1 Stream/Category Specific Questions 70 70 Composite time of 60 minutes
2 Banking, Present Economic Scenario & General Awareness 30 30
Category Qualifying Marks - Written Test Qualifying Marks - Interview
No. of candidates called for Interview 4 times the number of vacancies (category-wise)
Negative Marking No penalty for wrong answers

The selection process consists of an Online Examination followed by an Interview. Merely satisfying the eligibility norm does not entitle a candidate to be called for the test or interview. Candidates qualified in the online test will be called for interview to the extent of 4 times the number of vacancies. Final merit will be based on the combined performance in Online Test and Interview in the ratio of 70:30. In case of equal marks
candidates senior in age will be placed higher in the merit list.
how_to_apply: Candidates can apply only online from 30-04-2026 to 25-05-2026 at the link https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/cbiagmapr26/. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates should scan their photograph (200x230 pixels, 20-50 KB), signature (140x60 pixels, 10-20 KB), left thumb impression (240x240 pixels, 20-50 KB), and handwritten declaration (800x400 pixels, 50-100 KB). A valid personal email ID and contact number must be kept active till completion of the recruitment process. Not more than one application should be submitted by any candidate. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

An indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed by the selected candidate to indemnify the Bank in case such candidate leaves the service within 3 years of selection. Candidates must have a minimum CIC (Credit Information Company) score of 650 with no credit default or financial indiscipline as on the date of document verification.
Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview (Original + Self-attested copies):
Original Marks Sheet & Provisional Certificate / Degree Certificate issued from University
Experience Certificates as per eligibility requirements
Caste / PwBD (Divyangjan) / Caste Validity Certificate (wherever applicable)
OBC Certificate with Non-Creamy Layer clause (for OBC candidates)
EWS Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates)
No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Govt./PSU/Nationalized Banks)
Colour passport size photograph (same as uploaded in application form)
important_instructions: Only one application per candidate - multiple applications will result in forfeiture of fee paid for other registrations. Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn and fee once paid is neither refunded nor held in reserve. Candidates must ensure eligibility before applying - the Bank reserves the right to reject applications at any stage. Minimum CIC score of 650 is mandatory with no credit default in the credit report. Indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed if selected (valid for 3 years of service). Probation period of 2 years from the date of joining. Initial posting will be at Mumbai and thereafter transferable anywhere in India as per bank's transfer policy. Government/PSU/Nationalized Bank employees must produce NOC from employer at the time of interview. OBC candidates in 'Creamy Layer' will be treated as 'General Category'. No Travelling Allowance is payable to candidates called for interview. However, unemployed SC/ST candidates will be paid 2nd Class Rail/Ordinary Bus fare on production of travel evidence. Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification. Any resulting dispute shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of Courts situated at Mumbai. Candidates are advised to regularly visit the Bank's website www.centralbank.bank.in for updates under Career section. All decisions of the Bank regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding on all candidates.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 is 25 May 2026, and fee payment can also be done up to this date.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released for CBI AGM Specialist 2026?
faq.answer: The bank has notified a total of 15 vacancies for Assistant General Manager (Scale V) in Specialist Category across Risk Management, Finance & Accounts and Credit streams.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist posts?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 33 years and the maximum age is 45 years as on 31 March 2026, with relaxations for reserved categories as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q4. What is the selection process for CBI AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of an online examination for 100 marks followed by an interview for 100 marks, and the final merit is prepared with 70 percent weightage to the online test and 30 percent to the interview.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any negative marking in the Central Bank of India AGM Specialist online exam?
faq.answer: No, there is no penalty for wrong answers in the online examination for AGM Specialist posts, so candidates can attempt all questions without fear of negative marking.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist 2026?
faq.answer: SC, ST, PwBD and women candidates need to pay Rs. 175 as intimation charges, while all other candidates including General, OBC and EWS must pay Rs. 850 as application fee plus intimation charges.

faq.question: Q7. What qualifications are required for AGM – Finance & Accounts in Central Bank of India?
faq.answer: For AGM – Finance & Accounts, candidates must be full‑time graduates who have passed the final ICAI examination and are members of ICAI, with at least 10 years’ experience in a scheduled commercial bank and 7 years at head or administrative office in core finance functions.

faq.question: Q8. Is work experience mandatory for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist posts?
faq.answer: Yes, all AGM Specialist posts require a minimum of 10 years’ officer‑level experience in banking or specified domains, along with stipulated years in Risk, Finance or Credit in large scheduled commercial banks with more than 2000 branches.
